stem: The second-order linear homogeneous equation \( y'' + ([[b]])y' + ([[c]])y = 0 \) has characteristic equation \( r^{2} + ([[b]])r + ([[c]]) = 0 \), which has two distinct real roots. Which of the following is a root?
solution: Substituting \( y = e^{rt} \) gives the characteristic equation \( r^{2} + ([[b]])r + ([[c]]) = 0 \), which factors as \( (r - [[p]])(r - [[q]]) = 0 \). Its roots are \( [[p]] \) and \( [[q]] \); one such root is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This flips the sign of a root, a common error when reading \( (r - [[p]]) = 0 \).
This flips the sign of the other root.
This is the sum of the roots \( -([[b]]) \), not a root itself.
This is the product of the roots \( [[c]] \), not a root itself.
This uses the coefficient \( [[b]] \) as if it were a root.
This uses the coefficient \( [[c]] \) as if it were a root.
This is one more than a root.
This is one less than a root.

stem: The characteristic equation \( r^{2} + ([[b]])r + ([[c]]) = 0 \) of \( y'' + ([[b]])y' + ([[c]])y = 0 \) has two distinct real roots \( r_1 \) and \( r_2 \). What is \( r_1 + r_2 \)?
solution: By Vieta's formulas for \( r^{2} + ([[b]])r + ([[c]]) = 0 \), the sum of the roots is \( r_1 + r_2 = -([[b]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports the coefficient \( [[b]] \) directly, forgetting the negative sign in Vieta's formula.
This confuses the sum with the constant term \( [[c]] \).
This confuses the sum with the negative of the product of the roots.
This is the product of the roots, not their sum.
This has the wrong sign on the product of the roots.
This is one more than the correct sum.
This is one less than the correct sum.

stem: The characteristic equation \( r^{2} + ([[b]])r + ([[c]]) = 0 \) of \( y'' + ([[b]])y' + ([[c]])y = 0 \) has two distinct real roots \( r_1 \) and \( r_2 \). What is the product \( r_1 r_2 \)?
solution: By Vieta's formulas for \( r^{2} + ([[b]])r + ([[c]]) = 0 \), the product of the roots is \( r_1 r_2 = [[c]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign on the constant term.
This confuses the product with the coefficient \( [[b]] \).
This confuses the product with the negative of the sum of the roots.
This is the sum of the roots, not their product.
This has the wrong sign on the sum of the roots.
This is one more than the correct product.
This is one less than the correct product.

stem: For the equation \( y'' + ([[b]])y' + cy = 0 \), one root of the characteristic equation \( r^{2} + ([[b]])r + c = 0 \) is known to be \( r_1 = [[k]] \), and the equation has two distinct real roots. What is the other root \( r_2 \)?
distractor_rationales: This drops the negative sign from Vieta's sum-of-roots formula.
This subtracts in the wrong order.
This just negates the known root, ignoring the coefficient \( [[b]] \) entirely.
This reports the coefficient \( [[b]] \) itself as the other root.
This divides instead of using the sum-of-roots relation.
This is one more than the correct root.
This is one less than the correct root.
